materialChanges: Replaced the overbroad single evidence bridge with discrete claims for ratios, proportions, part-whole percentages, relative percent change, percentage-point difference, and relative-change domain boundaries.

Added first-edition OpenStax, NIST measurement-scale, and UK Ministry of Justice records; corrected the existing NIST page version; and rebuilt every learner-visible source note around exact claim support.
Restricted ordinary percent change to positive ratio-scale baselines with a meaningful zero and explained why zero, negative, and arbitrary-origin baselines need different handling.
Converted the diagnostic, worked retrieval, practice, transfer, and synthesis prompts to semantic check blocks with an attempt-before-feedback sequence and actionable recovery.

Added a Celsius boundary item, required substitution in the proportion item, aligned rubric criteria, and replaced descriptive acceptable-response entries with literal student response strings or empty rubric-scored arrays.

Expanded the glossary and attribution record for the new domain terms, factual sources, original synthesis, and fresh-finalizer provenance without changing lesson identity, version, status, or source-confidence state.

reviewDispositions.rationale: Added authoritative OpenStax, NIST, and Ministry of Justice records; split the central teaching into six scoped claims with direct or synthesis support; updated all same-scene source notes; and recorded the NIST page's August 18, 2025 update.

The concept, glossary, synthesis, claims ledger, and new assessment item now limit ordinary percent change to positive ratio-scale baselines and explicitly handle zero, negative, and arbitrary-origin Celsius cases.

Independent recomputation confirmed the reported 3:7 ratio, 30% and 41.666...% shares, 11.666... percentage-point increase, 38.888...% relative increase, and reverse check; the numerical key was retained.
The starting diagnostic is now a named check block followed by concrete answers, explanations, and prerequisite recovery guidance.
All four practice prompts now appear in separate semantic check blocks before a consolidated feedback section, so learners are instructed to complete every attempt before answers appear.
The transfer task is now a semantic check that requires all calculations and a check before the separately headed complete-attempt feedback.
Added item-percent-change-boundary and its rubric to assess whether the learner rejects an origin-dependent Celsius percentage and selects an absolute or justified domain-specific comparison.

Replaced meta-level acceptableResponses for the whole and percentage-comparison items with literal response variants, left the rubric-scored transfer array empty, and confirmed every retained acceptable response is a JSON string.
Moved the proportional-assumption explanation inside its check block and labelled it as feedback after an attempt, preserving progressive check semantics and a complete static reading path.
Converted item-proportion to short answer, required substitution in the prompt and answer, added actual response variants, and split its rubric into solution and verification criteria.

The reviewed candidate identity, scene order, objectives, and file mappings were valid; the final revision preserved lesson ID PREM-QNT-003, version 0.1.0, draft status, risk tier, ordered scene manifest, and file mappings.

evidenceChecked: Read all seven final scene sources in manifest order and confirmed one level-one heading per scene, logical subordinate headings, semantic source order, and no position-dependent routing.

Confirmed every formative interaction is a labelled check with textual instructions, an untimed non-drag response route, feedback after an attempted response, and a static reading path that survives reflow and print.
Confirmed equations and quantitative relations are also explained in words, and no distinction relies only on color, sound, motion, hover, pointer gesture, animation, or timing.

Confirmed the pack contains no figures, diagrams, audio, video, datasets, third-party assets, remote embeds, raw HTML, JavaScript, unsafe active content, or executable lesson code requiring an alternative modality or asset licence.

Checked every claim-to-scene-to-source relationship and learner-visible source note; all cited source IDs resolve to complete records with exact locators, dates, versions, uses, rights bases, and agent-access observations.

Checked the current public NIST, OpenStax, and GOV.UK rights or access routes used for the retained sources; they are used only for facts in independently written explanations, examples, prompts, and assessments.

Confirmed attribution identifies the accountable principal, original authoring agent, both advisory review artifacts, fresh finalizer run and digest, CC BY 4.0 lesson licence, fictional settings, original synthesis, and absence of reused source expression or media.

Confirmed the lesson contains no personal, learner, patient, credential, secret, clinical-advice, treatment, unsafe-experiment, or privacy-sensitive data and makes no claim of measured effectiveness, credit, publication, or competence.
limitations: The scoped run did not render the lesson or exercise a physical keyboard, screen reader, 200% zoom, narrow viewport, reduced-motion setting, or non-web export; equivalent access was audited from the canonical source only.

The operator prohibited reading site schemas and running npm, so formal JSON Schema, strict source-preflight, renderer, and repository validation remain required before merge; only JSON parsing and source-level integrity checks were run here.

Source support, displayed licence, and public access conditions were checked on current public pages without archived snapshots; GitHub COMMENTED transport equivalence was supplied by the operator rather than independently queried; agent-access records and run provenance are disclosed attestations, not independent verification.
The rights audit applies the repository's conservative reuse policy and is not a jurisdiction-specific legal opinion.

rationale: The exact original candidate and both eligible advisory reviews were read. The standard gate has two request-changes inputs, zero approvals, the required academic and learning-design roles, two distinct disclosed runs and providers, and one accountable principal; no additional proposed review was counted. All eleven indexed findings are disposed. Every substantive academic and learning-design defect was incorporated in one coherent revision, while the two positive note findings required no change. Manual JSON, mapping, rubric, arithmetic, field-length, source-note, semantic-source, privacy, security, accessibility, and rights checks passed. No reviewer is represented as approving the final edit, principal-level independence is not claimed, and formal repository validation remains an explicit operator condition. On that evidence, the final content is suitable for a merge decision once the placeholder stamping and required automated gates pass.
dissent: Both advisory reviews requested changes against the original candidate, and neither reviewer inspected or approved the finalizer's revision; this finalizer is accountable for the incorporated changes and final audit.

The learning-design review declared principal-level conflict because github:VSBDev operates the founding-stage runs; distinct agent runs and declared providers are attested, not verified independence.
conditions: Before merge, the accountable operator must replace PENDING-FINAL-COMMIT with the exact final content commit and replace all four RUNTIME-STAMPED identity fields from the runtime banner without changing teaching content.

Before merge, the operator must run the unchanged pack schemas, strict source preflight, renderer and repository validation required by project policy; any failure blocks merge until repaired within an authorized workflow.

After finalCommit is recorded, the maintainer may make only the separately authorized publication transition for lesson status, source confidence, and retained claim reviewStatus fields, then rerun the protected checks.

adjudicator.displayName: VSBDev — accountable operator, fresh finalizer and adjudicator role
